pdfs.title: OPERATIVE REPORT
pdfs.facility: St. Ambrose Medical Center — 400 Chew St, Allentown PA

pages.heading: Patient: Cole, Jasmine  DOB 02/14/1997  Date of surgery: 08/12/2024
lines: Surgeon: M. Okafor, MD, FACS   Assistant: L. Tran, PA-C
Anesthesia: General endotracheal
PREOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Stricturing ileocecal Crohn's disease with
recurrent partial small bowel obstruction, refractory to medical therapy.
POSTOPERATIVE DIAGNOSIS: Same.
PROCEDURE PERFORMED: Laparoscopic ileocecal resection with primary
stapled side-to-side (functional end-to-end) ileocolic anastomosis;
lysis of adhesions (approx 25 minutes); APPENDECTOMY performed as part
of the ileocecal specimen.
FINDINGS: 12 cm segment of thickened, strictured terminal ileum with
creeping fat and a fibrotic stricture 5 cm proximal to the ileocecal
valve. Moderate omental and pelvic adhesions. Cecum grossly involved at
the valve. No perforation, no abscess, no skip lesions palpated in the
proximal small bowel (run from ligament of Treitz).

pages.heading: OPERATIVE REPORT (CONTINUED)  PAGE 2/2
lines: TECHNIQUE (SUMMARY): 4-port laparoscopic approach. Adhesiolysis as
above. Ileocolic pedicle divided with vascular stapler. Terminal ileum
divided 3 cm proximal to diseased segment; ascending colon divided at
the cecal-ascending junction. Specimen (terminal ileum, cecum, and
APPENDIX en bloc) removed via Pfannenstiel extraction site.
Side-to-side stapled ileocolic anastomosis created; common enterotomy
closed. Anastomosis widely patent, pink, no tension, no leak on
inspection.
ESTIMATED BLOOD LOSS: 50 mL.  SPECIMEN: Ileocecal resection including
appendix, to pathology.  COMPLICATIONS: None.
PATHOLOGY (ADDENDUM 08/19/2024): Chronic active ileitis with
transmural inflammation and fibrostenosis, consistent with Crohn's.
Margins free of active disease. Appendix with fibrous obliteration,
no acute inflammation. 14 lymph nodes, reactive.
DISPO: Discharged POD 3, uncomplicated. Plan postoperative biologic
prophylaxis per GI (adalimumab + azathioprine started 10/2024).

pdfs.title: COLONOSCOPY REPORT (OUTSIDE RECORD, 12/18/2025)
pdfs.facility: Keystone Endoscopy Center — 850 Market St, Philadelphia PA

pages.heading: Patient: Cole, Jasmine  DOB 02/14/1997  Procedure date: 12/18/2025
lines: Endoscopist: P. Ramanathan, MD   Indication: Postoperative Crohn's
surveillance, s/p ileocecal resection 08/2024. Prep: Good (BBPS 8).
EXTENT: Cecal pouch/anastomosis reached; neo-terminal ileum intubated
to 10 cm.
FINDINGS:
Ileocolic anastomosis: Multiple aphthous ulcers plus several larger
ulcers (>5 mm) involving the anastomotic ring and extending into the
neo-terminal ileum. Mild luminal narrowing, scope traversed easily.
Neo-terminal ileum: Patchy erythema, aphthae, and 3 larger ulcers
within the distal 10 cm. Colon: Normal mucosa throughout. Rectum:
Normal.
IMPRESSION: Endoscopic postoperative recurrence — RUTGEERTS i2b
(lesions confined to anastomosis plus >5 aphthous ulcers in the
neo-terminal ileum with larger ulcers).
BIOPSIES: Anastomosis and neo-terminal ileum — chronic active ileitis,
no dysplasia, no granulomata, CMV immunostain negative.
RECOMMENDATIONS: Optimize medical therapy (check anti-TNF level and
antibodies; escalate or switch class per levels). Repeat ileo-
colonoscopy in 6-12 months. Avoid NSAIDs. Report faxed to GI office.
